basic_details.short_information: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre has released the official recruitment notification for 2 Medical Officer posts on an ad-hoc basis through walk-in interview mode. Candidates with MS/MD/DNB in Ophthalmology are eligible to attend the interview for this latest medical job opportunity at BARC. The maximum age limit is 40 years, and selected candidates will receive an approximate salary of Rs. 1,20,000/- per month along with other applicable benefits as per institute rules.

selection_process: Shortlisting based on highest percentage in MS/MD/DNB (if more than 10 candidates) Interview

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to attend the walk-in interview for BARC Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) posts?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview for the BARC Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) posts is scheduled for 19 May 2026. Candidates are advised to reach the venue well in advance.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) post?
faq.answer: The required educational qualification for the Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) post is MS/MD/DNB in Ophthalmology. Ensure you possess this degree to be eligible.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) position?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) position is 40 years. There might be age relaxations for specific categories as per government norms.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary offered for the Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) post?
faq.answer: Selected candidates for the Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) post will receive an approximate salary of Rs. 1,20,000/- per month.

faq.question: Q5. How many vacancies are available for the Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) post?
faq.answer: There are a total of 2 vacancies for the Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) post announced by BARC.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for the BARC Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) recruitment?
faq.answer: As per the notification, there is no application fee for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the BARC Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) recruitment?
faq.answer: The selection process involves shortlisting based on the highest percentage in MS/MD/DNB (if more than 10 candidates) followed by an interview.

faq.question: Q8. Where will the walk-in interview be conducted?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview will be conducted at the Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC) office in Mumbai, Maharashtra. The exact address and timing will be in the official notification.

faq.question: Q9. What documents should I carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: You should carry all original documents, one set of self-attested photocopies, ID proof, educational certificates, and category certificates (if applicable).

faq.question: Q10. Is this a permanent or ad-hoc position?
faq.answer: The job type for this Medical Officer (Ophthalmology) position is Ad-hoc.
